step_by_step_reasoning: The architecture is split into three main workflows: the fast bidding workflow, the log-archiving & conversion workflow, and the tracking workflow (hot and cold paths). In Flow 0 (Bidding), a user visits a site (www), initiating a 100ms subasta auction processed by EC2 Spot (Bidding) instances. Bid details are saved with an ID to ElastiCache (Redis). In Flow 1 (Archiving), EC2 Bidding flushes raw JSON logs to S3 every minute. S3 upload triggers a Lambda function to convert JSON to Parquet format and write it back. In Flow 2 (Tracking), user interaction triggers events on EC2 Spot (Tracking) instances. For hot events (< 1 hour), the tracking layer fetches data from ElastiCache. For older events, they are enqueued into SQS, triggering an Athena query over cold S3 logs to reconstruct the match.

graph.name: Smadex Real-Time Programmatic Ad Bidding and Tracking Architecture
graph.notes: Real-time programmatic bidding platform handling 1 million auctions/sec under 100ms constraint. Built on EC2 Spot Instances with a hot cache layer (ElastiCache Redis) and an SQS + Athena on S3 cold query layer to balance performance and storage cost optimization.

nodes.name: User
nodes.notes: Represented by the green stick figure on the left.

nodes.name: www
nodes.notes: Web icon representing the external website/app visited by users.

nodes.name: EC2 SPOT (Bidding)
nodes.notes: Top EC2 instance icon in the EC2 SPOT group representing the bidding layer.

nodes.name: EC2 SPOT (Tracking)
nodes.notes: Bottom EC2 instance icon in the EC2 SPOT group representing the tracking layer.

nodes.name: ELASTICACHE
nodes.notes: ElastiCache (Redis) icon used as the fast hot-data memory cache layer.

nodes.name: S3
nodes.notes: S3 bucket icon representing the cold-data layer storage.

nodes.name: LAMBDA
nodes.notes: Lambda icon at the top right representing the function that converts files to Parquet.

nodes.name: SQS
nodes.notes: SQS queue icon used to buffer old tracking requests.

nodes.name: ATHENA
nodes.notes: Athena query engine icon representing search over cold-storage.

edges.notes: User visits a website or application triggering an ad auction.
The auction request is sent to the bidding layer (under a 100ms constraint).
Saves the auction bid details in Redis with a unique ID.
Flushes raw JSON bid logs to S3 on a minute interval.
S3 file upload triggers the Lambda conversion function.
Lambda saves the optimized Parquet format files back into S3.
User interactions with ads are tracked by sending events to the tracking layer.
The tracking layer reads matching bid information using the unique ID.
Enqueues event lookups older than one hour.
Triggers a Lambda execution to run search queries in Athena.
Athena queries raw log data from S3 partitioned by minute.
